headline: In Dubai, Iranian entrepreneurs are caught in the crossfire: 'Iran has become a black hole'
description: Long established in the financial stronghold of the United Arab Emirates, the community now fears reprisals against relatives who remain in Iran.

headline: Jewish settlers escalate West Bank terrorist attacks under the guise of war with Iran
description: Within just one week, Jewish extremists have killed five Palestinians and wounded 15. While these acts are sometimes condemned by the army or the government, in practice, Israeli authorities turn a blind eye.

headline: Far right lays trap for Dati in Paris mayoral race
description: Rachida Dati, the conservative candidate for mayor of Paris, finds herself in an uncomfortable position as Eric Zemmour's party calls for an alliance in the second round.

headline: PSG is struggling, but not yet out of the running
description: Paris Saint-Germain, which faces Chelsea on Wednesday in the round of 16 of the Champions League, has lost the dominance it displayed last season. Nevertheless, the current campaign is far from over, writes journalist Jérôme Latta.

headline: 'The tendency to treat a local election as a national test may seem surprising'
description: With just 14 months to go before the 2027 presidential election, France's municipal elections are seen as a major political test by candidates seeking momentum, explains Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

description: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

headline: One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
description: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.

headline: Fashion's brand ambassadors step into an age of emancipation
description: For a long time, contracts between stars and fashion houses forbade them from appearing dressed in a rival label. Today, thanks to awards ceremonies and prestigious premieres, a new trend of increasing collaborations is emerging, shaking up the relationship between luxury and celebrities.

headline: Dominique Eddé, Lebanese writer: 'The present moment is overwhelming; it is one of destruction. All that remains is to think and to stand tall'
